mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE breaches 6.0%, or the signal's hit rate drops below 40% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which amplifies downside risk for corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in real personal spending exceeding 2.0 percent signals a sudden collapse in consumer demand, which rapidly deteriorates corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative drift following the spending drop threshold, or the signal's precision remains below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: During a steep yield curve tightening cycle, labor market resilience masks underlying cyclical fragility, leading to a sharp equity repricing once the labor data finally cracks, producing negative forward returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with resilient labor
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 60-day returns are positive or flat during periods matching the regime description, or if the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding capacity utilization and healthy business investment, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms a structural slowdown in employment growth, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline significantly after the dual-month payroll threshold breach, or the signal generates more false positives than true negatives in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising housing starts and accelerating real GDP growth indicates broad-based economic acceleration, which boosts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns through a demand feedback loop.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when GDP growth alone is used.
